summary: The AIM Problem Lists dataset contains 101 open problems from AIM workshops, with the visible activity concentrated in additive combinatorics (aim_addcombapp) and hypergraph Turán problems. Notably, every tracked problem shows 0 open and 1 resolved issue, all consisting of proof submissions, indicating a high resolution rate. The proof status metric shows a uniform distribution of 100 issues across quality bins q1–q10, suggesting an automated or batch verification pipeline rather than per-problem traction differences.
problems: Many resolved proofs are marked '(untitled)' (e.g., aim_addcombapp_0001, 0004, 0005, 001, 002, hypergraphturan_001), making it hard to assess content or correctness from metadata alone.
Inconsistent problem ID formatting mixes zero-padded (0001) and short (001) identifiers within the same family, risking duplicate or collision references.
Uniform 100-issue counts across all proof-status bins q1–q10 look like a placeholder or aggregate artifact rather than meaningful per-quality signal.
Each problem has exactly one resolved issue and zero open issues, leaving no visible backlog to prioritize and possibly hiding unverified or low-confidence proofs.
Only a small subset of the 101 problems is surfaced in the context, so coverage of the 80+ topics (spectral theory, number theory, geometry) is unrepresented here.
highlights: High resolution rate: all listed problems show resolved proof submissions with no lingering open issues.
Additive combinatorics is the most active area, with at least six resolved proof entries including the substantive '3APs in sets of small growth'.
Hypergraph Turán problems also show resolved activity, indicating progress across multiple distinct topic areas.
An apparent verification/proof pipeline (q1–q10 status tiers) is in place to grade or stage proof quality.

suggested_todos.title: Add titles and abstracts to all '(untitled)' resolved proofs to enable content review
Audit q1–q10 proof-status bins to confirm the uniform 100-issue counts are real, not a placeholder
Normalize problem ID formatting (zero-padding) across the aim_addcombapp family to prevent collisions
Verify the named result 'aim_addcombapp_0002: 3APs in sets of small growth' as the strongest near-publication candidate
Surface the remaining problems beyond the listed subset to assess full-dataset traction
